subject: Sociology
title: Race and Education
description: A quick, practical guide to Race and Education.

hook.text: Black and Latino students score lower on average. The question isn't their ability — it's their access.

buildup.text: Racial achievement gaps reflect centuries of segregation, underfunding, and institutional discrimination.

discovery.text: Schools in minority areas receive less funding, have fewer resources, and face higher teacher turnover.

twist.text: Stereotype threat — anxiety about confirming racial stereotypes — measurably lowers test performance alone.

climax.text: Racial gaps in education aren't about ability. They're about the accumulated weight of systemic disadvantage.

punchline.text: The gap measures the system, not the child.

quiz.question: What does stereotype threat cause?
options: Lower test performance due to anxiety about stereotypes
